Press

Boilerplate, facts, and the firm's quotation policy.

The firm publishes its methodology, validation standard, and curriculum in full and welcomes press citation. This page provides the canonical descriptors, leadership facts, and the firm's quotation and embargo policy. Press queries with deadlines should be sent directly to the address below.

Press contact · admin@power-in-numbers.net

§ 01 Quick facts

Firm

Power In Numbers

Type

Research and decision-engineering firm

Founded

2001

Founder & Principal

Jay Davis

Methodology, named publicly

CROWD POWERED — 2003

Validation standard

VERIDEX — 22 canonical gates, plus engine-specific extensions

Operating engines

Market Entry Intelligence Decision Protocol · VERIDEX BI USA · Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol

Curriculum

Eight-phase Power In Numbers University

Site

power-in-numbers.net

Press contact

admin@power-in-numbers.net

§ 02 Boilerplate descriptors

Three lengths, written for citation.

The firm provides three boilerplate descriptors — short, standard, extended — for press use. Each has been audited against the firm\'s canonical naming and is the form the firm prefers in published coverage.

Short (one line) — for byline notes

Power In Numbers is a research and decision-engineering firm operating under the CROWD POWERED framework and the 22-gate VERIDEX validation standard.

Standard (paragraph) — for press releases

Power In Numbers is a research and decision-engineering firm founded by Jay Davis in 2001. The firm operates three engines — the Market Entry Intelligence Decision Protocol for individual decision-makers, VERIDEX BI USA for US-domiciled firms and ventures, and the Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol for sovereign and multilateral principals — each calibrated to a distinct principal type and each clearing the firm's 22-gate VERIDEX validation standard. The firm publishes its methodology, validation standard, and curriculum in full at power-in-numbers.net.

Extended (two paragraphs) — for feature pieces

Power In Numbers is a research and decision-engineering firm founded by Jay Davis in 2001. Its operating framework — CROWD POWERED — was named publicly in 2003 and has remained the firm's discipline since. Its 22-gate VERIDEX validation standard is the audit standard every deliverable from any of the firm's three engines is required to clear.

The firm operates the Market Entry Intelligence Decision Protocol (the individual-grade engine), VERIDEX BI USA (the domestic firm-grade engine), and the Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol (the sovereign and multilateral engine). The firm has built three of its own ventures under the same standard — iMused (music attribution), Equity Guardians (homeowner advocacy), and the 144K Collective (philanthropic capital aggregation). Its public throughline runs from a 2011 Go Local Magazine profile of Dr. Janelle Thompson to a 2026 Market Entry Protocol Stage-3 reference deliverable presented at the Nexten Summit Accra.

§ 03 Quotation, embargo, and naming

Embargo policy.

The firm honours embargoes set by the convening institution (e.g., a multilateral conference, a ministry of finance, a host summit). Independent embargoes for press queries are accepted on a case-by-case basis and confirmed in writing.

Quotation policy.

Direct quotations from the firm's founder or principals are provided on the record. The firm does not provide off-the-record quotations. Background context — the firm's methodology, structure, and historical record — is on the public site at power-in-numbers.net and can be cited freely.

Methodology references.

Press references to CROWD POWERED, VERIDEX, the Market Entry Intelligence Decision Protocol, VERIDEX BI USA, or the Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol should match the firm's public canonical naming. The firm asks that the framework not be paraphrased to "PIN methodology" or similar — the names are part of the audit trail.

Deliverable references.

Where a deliverable is the subject of press coverage, the firm asks that the engine be named (the Market Entry Intelligence Decision Protocol, VERIDEX BI USA, or the Sovereign Intelligence Decision Protocol), the gate count cited (22 canonical, plus engine extensions where applicable), and the convening occasion attributed where relevant.

§ 04 Inquiries

Press, by deadline

admin@power-in-numbers.net — please include outlet, story, and deadline in the subject line.

Speaking and convenings

admin@power-in-numbers.net — convening institution, audience, and date in the subject line.

Academic citation

admin@power-in-numbers.net — paper title, journal or working-paper series, and target citation form.
